semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all sixteen assigned source notes and mapping limits, the eleven-field Authority Use Receipt schema and 3-valid/8-invalid corpus, the 2-valid/6-invalid SCIF commit probe, the 6-valid/7-invalid Resource Budget corpus and its security-cost control, all 22 SecurityKernel theorem declarations under four manifest targets, the post-v2.1 36-transaction governed-work outcome, every manifest atom, every prose scanner candidate, the sequence diagram, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep replaced a generic handle-and-compartment argument with a versioned threat-model and authority-use transaction; separated literal-secret exposure from semantic inference and authority abuse, handles from enforced leases, sanitization from declassification, SCIF naming from declared isolation grade, record validity from complete mediation, zeroization receipts from physical erasure, revocation from recovery and compensation, auditability from privacy, nominal role separation from dependency independence, fail-closed behavior from useful security, and finite proof from runtime refinement; installed strong comparators, multi-asset adversarial workloads, joint metrics, causal ablations, independent replication, transfer, and explicit promotion/refutation gates; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Tool-using and high-agency AI transactions involving secrets, private or licensed information, identity and session state, memory, model and policy artifacts, files, networks, code, money, deployment, physical or institutional effects, logs, backups, operators, vendors, and descendants across declared trust zones.
scope.environment: Current public schemas, deterministic fixtures, finite Lean records, one bounded repository-task campaign, and future frozen natural/adversarial real-model environments spanning local, cloud, browser, multimodal, and multi-agent execution; production security is outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned threat-model-bound authority-use transaction whose current implementation validates only explicit finite record fields and bounded routes; non-bypassability, isolation, semantic declassification, side-channel control, revocation, recovery, security, and transfer require runtime and independent empirical evidence.
scope.authority: Human/governance owners authorize threat model and policy; separate-enough identity, policy, approval, enforcement, context, declassification, audit, incident, revocation, recovery, privacy/rights, and Evidence State owners govern their exact surfaces; the model under judgment remains a proposer rather than sole authority.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; threat model, policy, authority tuple, assets, trust zones, channels, evaluator, monitor, isolation grade, budget, stop rules, outcome window, residual acceptance, and comparator identities must be frozen prospectively and versioned after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; sixteen reviewed source mappings; authority-use schema and 3/8 corpus; 2/6 SCIF commit probe; 6/7 budget corpus; 22 Lean declarations under four targets; post-v2.1 36-transaction outcome; future threat, lease, admission, effect, declassification, closure, attack, metric, ablation, replication, and transfer records.

assumptions: A useful but explicitly incomplete threat model, authority tuple, information-flow inventory, isolation grade, outcome window, and residual set can be frozen without implying open-world completeness.

Independent-enough implementations can observe literal and semantic disclosure, actual effects, side channels, declassification, revocation, recovery, availability, useful completion, privacy, and cost beyond subject self-report or record completeness.
Strong conventional vault/ACL, sandbox, zero-trust, control/data separation, and agent-security comparators can be implemented under matched tasks and budgets.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Mature secret managers, capability systems, zero-trust policy enforcement, sandboxes, information-flow control, progressive authorization, logging, incident response, and current agent-security defenses already cover the useful boundary; the proposed transaction adds correlated governance and record cost while no finite monitor can mediate open-world channels or reverse disclosure.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable admitted transaction violates any frozen authority, mediation, context, taint, isolation, declassification, egress, audit, revocation, recovery, privacy, rights, cost, or residual obligation; a required field has no exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the preregistered joint frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze the strongest classical, zero-trust, secret-management, capability-security, sandbox, information-flow, agent-benchmark, prompt-injection, incident-response, and project-lineage comparators without novelty laundering.
Justify assets, purposes, consent, rights, privacy, acceptable exposure, audit retention, denial, irreversible effects, compensation, residual acceptance, and incident authority.

Specify finite authority, flow labels, mediation, lifecycle, isolation declarations, declassification, revocation, closure, and non-promotion consequences while exposing abstraction and open-world limits.

Exercise actual policy decisions, substitutions, context admission, tool effects, egress, semantic outputs, logs, caches, expiry, revocation, descendants, recovery, compensation, and rejecting mutations.

Measure literal and semantic disclosure, unauthorized effects, attacks, false denial, missed help, declassification quality and utility, containment, revocation, audit privacy/completeness, availability, useful throughput, latency, compute, labor, recovery, compensation, and residuals.

Ablate each threat, authority, handle, context, mediation, isolation, control/data, taint, egress, declassification, revocation, audit, separation, accounting, and regression mechanism under matched work.

Repeat across models, assets, authorities, tools, runtimes, operators, local/cloud/browser environments, modalities, agents, attacks, side channels, organizations, and time with an independent implementation.

contrary_evidence: Classic protection principles, zero-trust architectures, capability systems, vaults, sandboxes, information-flow systems, and current agent-security work already cover substantial proposed mechanisms.
No finite inventory can guarantee complete mediation over unknown software, hardware, human, vendor, physical, semantic, covert, and descendant channels.
A handle can be stolen, replayed, redirected, correlated, or abused as authority even when secret bytes remain hidden.
Sanitization can preserve inferable or actionable protected semantics, and disclosure or external action may be irreversible.

The 36-transaction governed route eliminated its registered primary unsafe-release class but preserved only 2 useful releases, missed 4 exact attack-control rollbacks, and used correlated internal policy, observer, and promotion implementations.
The five mined project notes contain repeated examples where rich security types, checks, logs, or labels did not establish enforcement, isolation, durability, independence, or external capability.
known_confounds: Threat models, channel inventories, isolation grades, attack sets, disclosure thresholds, outcome windows, and residual definitions can be narrowed after seeing failures.
Literal-token scans can miss semantic disclosure; broad semantic judges can inflate false positives and share the target model's blind spots.
Fail-closed infrastructure or model failure can reduce both unsafe effects and useful completion without demonstrating a good security mechanism.
Nominally separate agents can share model family, training data, organization, infrastructure, prompts, metrics, and incentives.
Containers and local probes can omit browser, accelerator, network, vendor, operator, backup, timing, resource, and physical channels.
Security, privacy, availability, human labor, incident recovery, compensation, and rights costs can be excluded or shifted to another layer.
acceptance_criteria: Every frozen threat, asset, principal, trust zone, authority, context, taint, policy, monitor, isolation, egress, declassification, audit, revocation, recovery, cost, and residual field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed non-default consequence.

A preregistered real-model campaign meets joint usefulness, unsafe-disclosure/effect, declassification, privacy, availability, recovery, cost, causal-ablation, independent-replication, and transfer gates against every frozen strong comparator.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact assets, principals, authority tuple, policy, model, tool, runtime, data, environment, trust zones, adversary, channels, isolation grade, evaluator, monitor, epoch, outcome window, costs, and residuals.

Separate schema validity, policy decision, enforced mediation, literal exposure, semantic disclosure, authority abuse, isolation, declassification, commit, zeroization, expiry, revocation, recovery, compensation, support movement, and production outcome.

Unknown or failed identity, policy, telemetry, channel, isolation, evaluator, declassification, revocation, recovery, privacy, rights, cost, or closure remains denied, abstained, quarantined, aborted, residual, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.
Refute a mechanism benefit when its matched ablation preserves every frozen useful-security-privacy-availability-cost-recovery outcome or the full mechanism worsens the joint frontier.

Refute containment, complete mediation, superiority, or transfer language when an unregistered escape occurs, a simpler comparator dominates, or the effect fails declared independent replication and transfer.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise authority, context, information-flow, runtime-effect, custody, supply-chain, assurance, incident, privacy/rights, or evidence owner supersedes the atom and all consumers migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze one real multi-asset tool-use campaign, strong vault/ACL/sandbox/zero-trust/CaMeL/AgentDojo-style comparators, independent red team and policy/effect/declassification/audit implementations, exact environments and authority tuples, joint metrics, causal ablations, incident/recovery tests, replication, and transfer after P2/P3 semantics and runtime bindings exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, source mapping, a valid receipt, a blocked synthetic commit, a finite theorem, a red-team pass, or zero observed leaks does not establish complete mediation, secret safety, isolation, semantic non-disclosure, declassification quality, revocation, privacy, security, or transfer.

The current 3/8 authority corpus, 2/6 commit probe, 6/7 budget corpus, 22 Lean declarations, and 36-transaction bounded outcome establish only their exact record, route, mutation, and negative-result scopes.
No support state, deployment permission, route authority, conformance, novelty, release status, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted evidence transition.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.core.strongest_counterclaim: Existing secret management, zero trust, capability security, sandboxing, information-flow control, and incident response already provide the useful boundary; the proposed transaction is incomplete, bypassable, correlated, costly, and unable to reverse disclosure.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.core.falsifier: Any reachable admitted trace violates a frozen authority, flow, mediation, isolation, declassification, revocation, recovery, privacy, cost, or residual obligation, or any strong simpler comparator dominates the full joint frontier.
acceptance_criteria: Meet every frozen real-model usefulness, disclosure/effect, privacy, availability, recovery, cost, causal-ablation, independent-replication, and transfer gate against all strong comparators without hiding negative or null outcomes.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.proble.strongest_counterclaim: Representative deployed agent systems already keep every material information and authority flow inside adequately observed trust boundaries.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.proble.falsifier: A dated representative audit finds no material untrusted-data-to-authority, disclosure, bypass, revocation, recovery, privacy, or descendant boundary gap.
acceptance_criteria: Complete a dated comparator audit that preserves the strongest current systems and establishes only the exact remaining problem boundary.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.insuff.strongest_counterclaim: A properly composed vault, ACL, sandbox, zero-trust monitor, information-flow policy, output filter, audit log, and incident process supplies every named property at lower cost.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.insuff.falsifier: A frozen strong conventional composition implements and exercises every named property at equal or lower joint error, utility, privacy, availability, recovery, and governance cost.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce or audit strong current comparators before claiming a distinct missing boundary or mechanism advantage.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.minimu.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is self-authored schema and route theater plus a governance condition that became safe mainly by releasing almost nothing.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.minimu.falsifier: Any exact fixture, route, theorem, budget, governed-work, utility, rollback, dependency, or non-claim count fails clean recomputation or the prose generalizes beyond it.
acceptance_criteria: Cleanly reproduce 3/8 authority records, 2/6 commit routes, 6/7 budget records including the security control, 22 declarations under four targets, and 0 versus 24 unsafe, 2 useful, and 32 exact rollback outcomes across 36 transactions.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.beyond.strongest_counterclaim: The full transaction is dominated by simpler mature controls because open-world completeness is impossible and its governance overhead suppresses useful work without reducing real semantic or external-effect harm.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.beyond.falsifier: The full condition misses any frozen joint gate, any mechanism lacks its predicted causal signature, independent replication or transfer fails, or a strong simpler baseline dominates.
acceptance_criteria: Complete the preregistered multi-comparator, real-model, multi-asset, adversarial, causal, independently replicated, transfer campaign and meet every frozen joint gate.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem restates an explicit authorization premise and says nothing about whether a runtime obtains or enforces it.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.falsifier: The model admits substitution without its named authorization or cannot refine to the executable route without changing meaning.
acceptance_criteria: Audit proof necessity and connect the predicate to an executable enforcement trace with rejecting mutations in P2.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem restates a clearance comparison and does not prove context minimization, taint, isolation, or runtime admission.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.falsifier: The model admits insufficient clearance or cannot refine to an executable admission path without changing meaning.
acceptance_criteria: Audit proof necessity and bind clearance to real context admission, omitted-material residuals, and rejecting runtime mutations in P2.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.strongest_counterclaim: The route merely maps trusted Boolean fields to labels; it neither detects the fields nor models information flow, actual effects, observer error, or bypass.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.falsifier: A modeled record reaches an unsafe allow state or the route cannot represent an executable threat-model case without semantic distortion.
acceptance_criteria: Add countermodels, observer uncertainty, actual effect semantics, non-vacuity tests, and executable refinement in P2 before any mechanized-security language.

security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.strongest_counterclaim: Literal field checks over a toy commit cannot represent semantic disclosure, declassification quality, isolation, zeroization, side channels, or descendant effects.
security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.formal.falsifier: Any probe control reaches commit incorrectly or a realistic semantic leakage case cannot be represented without changing the target.
acceptance_criteria: Replace literal-only abstractions with explicit information labels, declassification authority, evaluator uncertainty, effect inventory, and executable refinement in P2/P3.

non_claims: This semantic sweep does not promote the chapter, prove novelty, or authorize deployment.
No source mapping, handle, SCIF label, schema, fixture, theorem, audit row, or blocked attack is counted as security evidence beyond its exact frozen scope.
Negative, null, utility-collapse, inexact-recovery, dependency, privacy, rights, and unknown-channel outcomes remain visible and cannot be relabeled as successful containment.
